owner_add:
The current number (%s) the bot is connected to is not added to the owners list.
Do you want to add this number to the owners list? (bot_config.json)
scan_qr: Scan the QR Code to connect the bot to your account:
qr_setup_info: Creates a QR Code for you to scan inside the WhatsApp client. Generally faster, but requires a device to run this bot and another to scan the code.
pairing_setup_info: Creates an 8-digit code for you to manually type inside the WhatsApp client. Slower and requires your phone number, but does not require another device.
pairing_code_needs_number:
To use the pairing code, you'll need to type your WhatsApp number below.
The number must have no spaces, hyphens, or a leading + sign.
(e.g., 12345678901)

tries_exceeded: Tried three times, but failed to connect on all of them. Deleting your session folder...

config_make_prefix_ask:
Select a prefix for BlossomBot to use. It's recommended that you make this prefix have ONLY ONE character. E.g.: "/", "!", ";", "*", ".", etc.
Default is "/"; Press ENTER without anything written to use it.
After settings up the bot, you can modify this with the /prefix command.

loading_strings_done: Done loading text string definitions, %s loaded on total

generic.incorrect_syntax: Incorrect syntax. Check *"%shelp %s"* for more info.

ping.help:
|ping is a command used to test the bot's response time and connectivity to WhatsApp's servers. It serves like a "are you alive?" check.
It responds you with the *latency* and *transmission speed*. Latency means the time that the bot took to recieve the message, and the transmission speed means the time the bot took to build and send a message.

help.no_help:
This command doesn't have a help page.
Well, not yet :)
help.no_help_no_exist: This command doesn't have a help page because it doesn't exist.
help.help:
*|help* is a command used to check useful information about a command. For example, you'll get info on how to use it, tips and tricks, and more.

Usage:
> ```|help <command name>```
help.captions:
> ```<argument>```
Optional argument - provides extra content but can be skipped.

> ```[argument]```
Necessary argument - the command will not run without it.

> ```--flag (long) / -f (short)```
Toggle settings that modify execution behavior - always optional.

menu.categories_2: To check the commands inside a category, run *"%smenu <category name>"*.
menu.help:
*|menu* displays all commands of this bot.
They are separated in categories, with each category containing it's own list of available commands.

Usage:
> ```|menu```
Displays all categories.

> ```|menu <category name>```
Displays all commands inside a category.

> ```|menu --all/-a```
Displays *all* commands in *all* categories in *one* message.¨

prefix.same_prefix: You're trying to change the prefix of the bot globally to *%s*.
prefix.same_prefix2: The global prefix *is already %s*. No changes made.
prefix.same_prefix_not_global: You're trying to change the prefix of this group to *%s*.
prefix.same_prefix2_not_global: Its prefix *is already %s*. No changes made.
prefix.changed_to_not_global: Changed prefix from *%s* to *%s* for *this group only*.
prefix.changed_to_reset: Prefix reset from *%s* to *%s* for *this group only*.
prefix.changed_to_reset_generic: Prefix reset to *%s* for *this group only*.
prefix.changed_to_not_global_2: Note that only *this group* will have its prefix changed, the rest will have its own.
prefix.changed_to_global_not_overriten_2: Note that this change only applies to groups without custom prefixes (which are not global).
prefix.help:
*|prefix* changes the bot prefix, globally or for specific groups.

Usage:
> ```|prefix [new prefix]```
Changes the prefix of the bot globally. (doesn't get changed in groups with a specific prefix which is not global)

> ```|prefix [new prefix] --override/-o```
Changes the prefix of the bot globally, and overrides *ALL* groups with a specific prefix which is not global to the new one.

> ```|prefix --reset/-r```
In case the group this was run on has a custom prefix that is not the global one, this will revert it back to the global one.

> ```|prefix [new prefix] --specific/-s```
Changes the prefix of the bot *specifically for this group*, making the prefix *not global*.¨
